noSubtitles: Back to your local model peasant
But that's okay, because everything is running smoothly..right?

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet explicitly says they are "resetting the usage limits for all plans," meaning rate/usage caps are available again now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Our Codex dashboards are showing increased rate of users hitting rate limits and since we don't fully understand why I have made the cautious decision of resetting the usage limits for all plans. Enjoy.

I also wanted to celebrate us finding a pocket of fraudulent accounts that we banned and have helped us regain some compute. The fight against abuse never stops, but it's important to mark the moment and make it a little shared victory.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet is about a repository being public / a code leak, not about usage limits, caps, or rate limits resetting.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: Whaaaa. Only realized now and apparently our repo was public since 11 months ago and noone told us?!

recentEvaluations.rationale: This is a personal reply with no mention of Codex, ChatGPT, limits, caps, or usage availability.
The tweet promotes a Codex plugin and says it works with a ChatGPT subscription; it does not mention limits, caps, or a reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
You can now experience the power of codex while the little orange thing keeps you company. Works with your ChatGPT subscription, just as everywhere else.

Clearly proof you can just build things. Have fun

recentEvaluations.tweetText: Growth of codex continues to outpace our prediction (maybe we are not super good at that) and we almost ran out of capacity three days in a row. We have ramped up more significantly ahead of next week.

recentEvaluations.rationale: A brief reply saying “Enhance”; it does not mention limits, caps, resets, or availability.
The post text is only a link and provides no explicit indication that any Codex or ChatGPT limit/reset occurred.
The tweet explicitly says Codex usage limits were reset across all plans and users can build unlimited things now.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Hello. We have reset Codex usage limits across all plans to let everyone experiment with the magnificent plugins we just launched, and because it had been a while! 

You can just build unlimited things with Codex. Have fun!

recentEvaluations.rationale: This is a conversational reply expressing enthusiasm and does not mention limits, caps, resets, or availability.
The tweet is a joke/comment about the Codex team using Codex and says nothing about limits, caps, or usage resetting.
The tweet discusses a Codex-related automation/project and says nothing about limits, caps, availability, or a reset.
recentEvaluations.tweetText: A fun one: I combined it with a skill and automation to physically print a one-page update on public discourse around Codex that I can read together with my morning coffee.

recentEvaluations.rationale: The tweet is about Codex plugins/integrations and account access, not about rate limits, caps, or usage resetting.
recentEvaluations.tweetText:
Boom. Plugins! Codex now has access to most things it couldn’t reach before and all super easy behind the same with you use for your ChatGPT account.

I use this for *everything*, from managing my calendar, triaging incoming bugs, keeping up to date with what everyone is doing at the company and so much more
